commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh Behold, he spreads his lightning upon it, and covers the depths of the sea. Literally, "the hinges "or poles, cardines. (Haydock) Hebrew, "roots "Aristotle (Meteor. ) and Hesoid (Theog. 727,) use the same term, (Calmet) to denote the fountains which supply the sea. (Haydock) Who ever discovered these deep recesses. Eliu describes a thunder-storm, when the sea is covered with darkness. He intimates that the pavilion of God, though hidden from us by the clouds, is not destitute of light. (Calmet) God inhabits light inaccessible.
